attempt_local_note: Attempt-local instance of the same schema (no closed-loop-attempt-state.v1 leaf exists for this B/C1/C2/C3 lineage). Unlike C1/C2 (verdict issued directly by the named human), this C3 verdict is issued by the orchestrator under the named human's recorded live delegation for routine same-class judgments. It supersedes the historical human_review:'pending' fields in provenance.json and regression.json by lineage; those files are left unmodified as historical evidence.

delegator.name: Choe Mun-yeong
delegator.delegation_quote_verbatim: 이 정도는 그냥 너가 판단해도 되는 수준
delegator.delegation_scope: routine same-class judgments (same defect class as the human-adjudicated Panel A sulfur-segment frame crossing) delegated to the orchestrator; recorded in adjudication.json

verdict_translation: Accepted as a development baseline for the two repaired accidental frame-crossing labels; overall polish/completeness is NOT claimed. The two labels left open and the one disclosed sub-threshold near_miss are accepted as known-open items.
scope_note: Development-baseline acceptance of the R5.2c bounded repair: the Panel F 'floating polymer cantilever' and 'driven electrode' labels moved (position/anchor only) so they no longer cross their frames (boundary crossings 6 -> 4). NOT release acceptance and NOT publication acceptance. Strict compile does not pass (pre-existing check_visual_clash failure identical to C1/C2, unrelated to this repair).
accepted_known_open_items: Panel E 'grounded substrate' left unrepaired (boxed between the left frame and the substrate rectangle; no position/anchor-only fit).
Panel F 'grounded source return' left unrepaired (clearing the right frame overlaps the HV wiring/motif and its own referent).
One sub-threshold untiered visual_clash near_miss on 'driven' (dark 0.019) from moving that label toward its own electrode-bar referent; leader still connects, after-PNG reads clean.
